new_classes.label_en: Distillation process from fermented plum, pear or cherry mash
new_classes.label_de: Destillationsprozess aus vergorener Pflaume-, Birnen- oder Kirschmaische
new_classes.definition_en: A spiritous distillation process in which spirit is produced exclusively by distillation from fermented mash of plums, pears or cherries.

new_classes.label_en: Plum, pear or cherry spirit quality
new_classes.definition_en: Quality of a spirit that is produced exclusively by distillation from fermented mash of plums, pears or cherries (Slivovitz, Pálinka, Kirsch, etc.).

new_classes.label_en: Plum, pear or cherry spirit, in containers holding ≤ 2 l (CN 2208 90 33)
new_classes.label_de: Pflaumenbranntwein, Birnenbranntwein oder Kirschbranntwein, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von höchstens 2 l (KN 2208 90 33)
new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength of less than 80% vol, being a spirit obtained exclusively by distillation from fermented mash of plums, pears or cherries, put up in containers holding 2 litres or less (CN code 2208 90 33).

coverage_explanation: The legal text specifies three key discriminating criteria for CN 2208 90 33: (1) the spirit must be plum, pear or cherry brandy (Pflaumenbranntwein, Birnenbranntwein, Kirschbranntwein); (2) it must be produced exclusively by distillation from fermented mash of plums, pears or cherries; (3) it must be presented in containers holding ≤ 2 l. All three criteria are captured: the PlumPearCherryFruitSpiritQuality quality class captures criterion (1), the PlumPearCherryFruitMashDistillationProcess process class captures criterion (2), and the SmallContainer restriction captures criterion (3). The UndenaturedEthylAlcoholLessThan80VolQuality restriction captures the parent heading's requirement (< 80% vol, undenatured) as it belongs under CN 2208. A minor gap (score -0.1) is that the legal note references subsidiary definitions for 'Pflaumen' (plums) and 'Kirschen' (cherries) via cross-reference to additional notes (note text cut off with 'E'), which cannot be fully modelled without the complete referenced note text.
